title: Phase 4 Review: Self, Time of Day & Colors

meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 3–4.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Rikizo and Yamakawa chat at the office before work about their morning routines.

lines.jp: やまかわさん、今朝は 何時に 起きましたか。
六時に 起きました。朝ごはんを 食べてから 来ました。
私も 毎朝 朝ごはんを 食べます。毎朝 起きられますか。
はい、起きられます。明るい 朝が 好きですよ。

items.question: What does Yamakawa say about mornings?
choices: Yamakawa can't wake up every morning
Yamakawa comes to work by train
items.explanation: B says 明るい朝が好きですよ — 'I like bright mornings.' The い-adjective 明るい (bright) modifies 朝 (morning) in attributive position.

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: Rikizo is at a clothing store asking a staff member about shirt colors.

lines.jp: すみません。シャツは 何色が ありますか。
赤い シャツと 青い シャツと 黒い シャツが あります。茶色も ありますよ。
私は 黒が 好きですが、青い シャツも 見られますか。
はい。色々な 色が あります。どうぞ。

items.question: What does Rikizo want to see at the store?
items.explanation: A says 私は黒が好きですが、青いシャツも見られますか — 'I like black, but can I also see a blue shirt?' The potential form 見られます means 'can see/can be seen.'

items.title: Conversation 3 (Casual)
items.context: Two close friends chat casually at a café about their morning habits.

lines.jp: 今朝、朝ごはんを 食べた？
食べなかったよ。毎朝 起きられないから。
私は 毎朝 朝ごはんが 食べられるよ。
いいね。今晩は よく 休もう。

items.question: Why didn't B eat breakfast this morning?
choices: Because B can't wake up every morning
Because B went to the café early
items.answer: Because B can't wake up every morning
items.explanation: B says 毎朝起きられないから — 'because I can't wake up every morning.' 起きられない is the potential negative (plain) form of 起きる: unable to wake up.

sections.title: Part 2: Grammar Scramble
sections.instructions: Construct the sentence by clicking the words in the correct order.

items.q: Every morning Rikizo eats breakfast and goes to work.
items.explanation: りきぞ + は (topic) + 毎朝 + 朝ごはん + を (object of 食べる) + 食べて (te-connector) + 会社に (destination) + 行きます. 毎朝 (every morning) can naturally appear sentence-initially too. が marks a subject in contrast, not the topic here. Use を to mark the food being eaten.

items.q: Tonight I want to drink green tea.
items.explanation: 今晩 + は (topic) + お茶 + を (object of 飲む) + 飲みたいです (want to drink). Use を to mark the thing being drunk. に marks destinations or recipient persons, not the thing consumed. 朝ごはん + 食べたいです would mean 'want to eat breakfast', which doesn't match 'green tea.'

items.q: My friend gave me a black shirt.
items.explanation: 友だち + が (subject/giver) + 私 + に (recipient) + 黒い + シャツ + を (object given) + くれました. くれる is used when someone gives something to the speaker (私). あげる is the reverse — the speaker gives to others. に marks the person who receives; を marks the thing given.

items.q: Various colors of shirts can be seen here.
items.explanation: ここ + で (location where action happens) + 色々な (various — na-adj attributive form) + 色の + シャツ + が (subject of potential) + 見られます (can see/be seen). で marks the place where you can see things. In potential expressions, the thing that can be experienced often takes が. 見ます is plain present without the sense of ability.

items.q: This morning's sky was very bright.
items.explanation: 今朝 + の (modifier: this morning's) + 空 + は (topic) + とても + 明るかったです. の connects 今朝 and 空 as a noun phrase. は marks the topic. 明るかったです is the correct past polite form of the い-adjective 明るい: the い changes to かった + です. 明るいでした is incorrect — い-adjectives do not attach でした directly to the dictionary form.

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — My Morning
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer the comprehension questions.

passage.jp: 私は 毎朝 六時に 起きます。
passage.en: I wake up at six every morning.

passage.jp: 朝ごはんを 食べてから、会社に 行きます。
passage.en: After eating breakfast, I go to work.

passage.jp: 今朝は 空が とても 明るかったです。
passage.en: This morning the sky was very bright.

passage.jp: 私は 明るい 朝が 大好きです。
今晩、友だちと 晩ごはんを 食べます。
passage.en: Tonight I will eat dinner with a friend.

passage.jp: 私は 黒い シャツと 青い シャツが 好きですが、赤い シャツも 見たいです。
passage.en: I like black shirts and blue shirts, but I also want to see red shirts.

questions.q: 私は 毎朝 何時に 起きますか。
questions.explanation: 私は毎朝六時に起きます — I wake up at 6 every morning. The answer 六時 (6 o'clock) is stated directly in the first sentence.

questions.q: 今朝の 空は どうでしたか。
questions.a: とても 明るかったです
choices: とても 明るかったです
あまり 明るく ありませんでした
空は 見えませんでした
questions.explanation: 今朝は空がとても明るかったです — this morning the sky was very bright. 明るかったです is the past polite form of the い-adjective 明るい (bright).

questions.q: 私は どの 色の シャツを 見たいですか。
questions.explanation: 赤いシャツも見たいです — I also want to see red shirts. The passage says I already like black and blue shirts (好きです), but want to see (見たい) red ones too.

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: [私]は 毎朝 朝ごはんを 食べます。
items.explanation: 私 (I / me) is read as watashi. This is the primary kun-reading. 私 (N4.3).

items.q: 今朝は とても [明るかった]です。
items.explanation: 明るい (bright) in past form is 明るかった, read as akarukatta. 明るい past = remove い, add かった. 明 (N4.3).

items.q: [朝ごはん]を 食べてから、学校に 来ます。
items.explanation: 朝ごはん (breakfast) is read as asagohan. 朝 (N4.3) + ごはん (hiragana until 飯 is taught in N4.6).

items.q: [今晩]は 友だちと 晩ごはんを 食べます。
items.explanation: 今晩 (tonight) is read as konban. 今 (N5.2) + 晩 (N4.3). Contrast with 今朝 (けさ, this morning).

items.q: 何[色]の シャツが 好きですか。
items.explanation: 色 (color) is read as iro in its kun-reading. The on-readings しょく and しき appear in compounds like 色彩. 色 (N4.4).

items.q: 青い シャツが [好き]ですよ。
items.explanation: 好き (like / fond of) is read as suki. The kanji 好 (N4.4) has on-reading こう and kun-reading す. In the word 好き, the reading is す + き.

items.q: [赤い] シャツを 見たいです。
items.explanation: 赤い (red) is read as akai. 赤 (N4.4). Contrast: 青い (aoi, blue), 黒い (kuroi, black), 白い (shiroi, white).

items.q: [黒い] シャツと 赤い シャツが あります。
items.explanation: 黒い (black) is read as kuroi. 黒 (N4.4). Contrast: 赤い (akai, red) and 茶色い (chairoi, brown) are common distractors for color vocabulary.

items.q: [週まつ]は 家で 晩ごはんを 食べます。
items.explanation: 週まつ (weekend) is read as shuumatsu. 週 (N5.6) means week; 末 is written in hiragana (まつ) since the kanji 末 is not formally taught. Contrast: 毎週 (まいしゅう, every week), 週間 (しゅうかん, a week-long period).

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: 毎朝 ______を 食べてから、会社に 行きます。
items.explanation: 朝ごはん (breakfast) fits: you eat breakfast in the morning before going to work. 晩ごはん is dinner — an evening meal. お茶 is a drink, not a meal you eat. シャツ is a shirt — you wear it, not eat it.

items.q: ______は 空が とても 明るかったです。
items.explanation: 今朝 (this morning) fits: the sky was very bright this morning. 今晩 (tonight) and 毎晩 (every night) refer to evening, when the sky is dark. 明日 (tomorrow) is future, but 明るかったです is past tense — these contradict each other.

items.q: 友だちは ______な 色が 好きです。赤も 青も 黒も 好きですよ。
items.explanation: 色々な (various) fits: the friend likes various colors — red, blue, and black are all listed. 色々 is a na-adjective and requires な before a noun. 明るい, 白い, and 新しい are い-adjectives and cannot take な; they also don't convey the meaning of 'various.'

items.q: ______は 家で 晩ごはんを 食べます。
items.explanation: 週まつ (the weekend) is the natural time expression here — on the weekend, you eat dinner at home. 今朝 (this morning) is a morning time, not when you have dinner. 朝ごはん is breakfast (a meal, not a time). お茶 is a drink, not a time expression.

items.q: 私は ______が 大好きです。毎朝 飲みます。
items.explanation: お茶 (green tea) fits: I love green tea and drink it every morning. 晩ごはん is dinner — you eat it, not drink it. シャツ is a shirt — you wear it. 朝ごはん is breakfast — you eat it; 飲む (drink) doesn't fit.

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle or verb form to complete the sentence.

items.q: 友だち ______ 私に 黒い シャツを くれました。
items.explanation: Use が to mark 友だち as the subject (the giver) of くれる. 友だちが私にくれた = 'my friend gave it to me.' を marks the direct object (黒いシャツ). に marks the recipient (私). が here shows who performed the giving action.

items.q: 私は 黒が 好きです ______ 青い シャツも 見たいです。
items.explanation: が after a predicate (好きですが) connects two contrasting clauses: 'I like black, but I also want to see blue shirts.' This が is a clause-linking conjunction expressing contrast. から after a predicate expresses reason ('because I like black') — which changes the meaning. まで means 'until/as far as.' に marks destinations or recipients.

items.q: ここでは 色々な 色の シャツが ______。
items.explanation: 見られます is the polite potential form of 見る: 'can be seen / can see here.' ここでは (here) sets the location. 見ます means 'I look/see' without expressing ability or availability. 見られました is past potential (could be seen). 見せます means 'I show (something to someone)' — a different verb.

items.q: 友だちは 色々 ______ 色が 好きです。
items.explanation: 色々 is a na-adjective. Na-adjectives use な when they precede a noun (attributive position): 色々な + 色 = 'various colors.' い-adjectives use い before nouns (not な). が marks a subject. で marks means or location — neither is an adjective connector.
